title: Czech consonants and háček letters
summary: Learn the consonants that differ most from English and avoid English-style aspiration on p, t, and k.
objectives: Recognize c, č, š, ž, ch, ř, and j
Pronounce p, t, and k without a strong puff of air
Read common Unit 1 words more predictably

sections.heading: Familiar consonants
sections.body: Many Czech consonants are close to English values, including b, f, v, m, d, s, and z. Their spelling is dependable.

sections.heading: Important Czech values
sections.body: Several letters or letter combinations need deliberate practice. A háček changes the sound of the base letter.

sections.heading: Less aspiration
sections.body: English p, t, and k often release a noticeable puff of air. Czech p, t, and k are normally produced with much less aspiration.

resources.title: Original lesson: Czech Consonants
resources.note: Source for the pronunciation categories and examples.

exercises.prompt: Which Czech letter is closest to English sh?
Type the Czech word for what.
exercises.explanation: Co begins with the ts-like Czech c.

exercises.prompt: Which letter normally sounds like English y in yes?
exercises.explanation: Czech j is pronounced like English y.
